page: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. This dedicated CPU configuration provides 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache, alongside support for AVX-512 vector extensions and AES cryptography.

A defining characteristic of this instance is its high memory density, offering 64.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 16.0 GB per core. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio is paired with 307 GB of local SSD storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average range. However, multi-core performance is constrained, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores rated as weak. Memory bandwidth also shows limitations, with large block read and write speeds categorized as weak. This indicates that while the instance provides substantial memory capacity, it is not designed for high-throughput parallel compute tasks.

Given its hardware profile, the E8-4ds_v4 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require a large memory footprint and local scratch storage without demanding intensive CPU processing. It is well-suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and enterprise applications that benefit from high memory-to-core ratios.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) and a 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is characterized by a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, providing 64.0 GB of RAM (16.0 GB per core) alongside 307 GB of local SSD storage. While single-core benchmarks for CPU and text processing perform in the average tier, multi-core compute and memory bandwidth benchmarks are relatively weak. This hardware profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, ideal for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory-to-core density and local SSD storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core memory density
307 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core CPU and text processing performance
Weak multi-core compute and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Suited for databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy applications
